picks.title: Passover Seder Dinner
picks.why: Easter tradition: family-friendly Passover Seder dinner celebrates spring holiday at beautiful Santa Clara University campus.

picks.why: Classic movie screening of The Princess Bride—timeless family entertainment ideal for spring break movie day in Palo Alto.

picks.why: Jessica Monette's art exhibition explores family stories and identity—engaging, thought-provoking display for curious kids and parents.

picks.title: Pelicans, Herons, and Egrets, Oh My!
picks.why: Wildlife photographer Joan Sparks guides discovery of elegant wading birds—perfect outdoor learning adventure for nature-loving families.

picks.title: Furry Reading Buddies for Grades K-5
picks.why: Furry Reading Buddies program: kids boost confidence reading aloud to friendly dogs—playful, encouraging spring break activity in Saratoga.

picks.why: Earth Heroes mission teaches recycling through games, crafts, activities—fun STEM learning helping kids protect the planet this spring.

picks.why: San Francisco Shakespeare Festival performs Julius Caesar—outdoor theatrical drama offering culture and excitement for teens and families.

picks.title: Freedom Readers Book Club (Grades 5-8)
picks.why: Freedom Readers graphic novel book club celebrates diverse stories for grades 5-8—engaging spring break reading community during week two.

picks.title: Little Learners STEAM for Ages 3-5
picks.why: Little Learners STEAM program for ages 3-5 nurtures curiosity through hands-on activities—perfect for younger kids during spring break.

picks.title: Page Turners Book Club for Grades 4-5
picks.why: Page Turners Book Club (grades 4-5) discusses The City of Ember—exciting community reading experience exploring adventure and imagination.

picks.title: Family Craft: Yarn Weaving
